market_context: Market sentiment on X/Twitter is cautiously optimistic, with many users discussing potential rate cut signals from the Fed and strong quarterly earnings expectations. Pre-market futures for S&P 500 and Nasdaq are up slightly by 0.3% and 0.4% respectively, while notable movers include tech giants like AAPL and NVDA. No major news events are scheduled, but end-of-quarter portfolio rebalancing could drive volatility.

predictions.reasoning: Positive pre-market futures for S&P 500 (+0.3%) suggest upward momentum, supported by optimistic X/Twitter chatter about potential Fed rate cuts. Additionally, historical end-of-quarter buying trends could push SPY higher by close.

AAPL is a notable pre-market mover, up 0.5%, likely due to positive sentiment on X/Twitter about upcoming product rumors. Web searches indicate strong options flow with heavy call buying for April expiries, signaling bullish expectations.

NVDA shows pre-market strength (+0.6%) and positive mentions on X/Twitter regarding AI sector growth. Web data highlights increased short interest, but bullish options flow suggests potential for a short squeeze by day’s end.
